rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe, efficient exploration by preferring forward movement when clear, and using deterministic, single-action recovery strategies when obstacles are detected. Helper states (commit/recovery/scan) ensure atomic actions and prevent oscillations. Each state is total on uncontrollables, and only one controllable is enabled per state (except obs_front, which allows both rotate and scan for robust escape).
strategy: Prefer forward movement in clear state for efficient coverage.
On front obstacle, use clockwise rotation or full scan to escape, avoiding direct backward movement to reduce oscillation.
On left obstacle, rotate clockwise to clear the left side.
On right obstacle, rotate counterclockwise to clear the right side.
Commit states ensure atomic execution of actions, always returning to a perception state after action.
Full scan (scan_full) is available as a robust escape from dead-ends or ambiguous situations.
All states are total on uncontrollable events, ensuring robust reaction to sensor changes.
Only one controllable is enabled per state (except obs_front, which allows both rotate and scan for robust escape).
No infinite controllable loops; all commit/scan states return to perception/decision states.
No oscillatory behavior: rotation direction is fixed for each obstacle side.
